messages.content:
You are a careful evaluator of literary affect for a benchmark comparison study.
Follow the requested evaluation target exactly.
Return valid JSON only.
Do not output markdown fences.

Task:
Read the literary text below and rate four emotions on a 0 to 100 integer scale.

Evaluation target:
- target_mode: reader_side
- target_label: reader-side post-reading affect
- focus: Rate emotions felt by a reader after reading the whole text.

Output language:
English

Dimensions and definitions:
- interest: How funny, entertaining, or intellectually enjoyable the text feels
- surprise: How unexpected, novel, or revelatory the text feels
- sadness: How sad, melancholic, or poignant the text feels
- anger: How much anger, indignation, or displeasure the text evokes

Scoring rules:
- use integers only
- 0 means the emotion is not present at all
- 50 means moderately present
- 100 means extremely strongly present
- rate the text as a whole after reading it
- keep each reason brief and grounded in the text

Return exactly one JSON object matching the schema.
Use persona_id "p0" and language "en" in the JSON object.

Text identifier: T1
Title: The Pocket Watch
Author: Kyusaku Yumeno
Language of this prompt: en

Text:
A pocket watch had fallen behind a chest of drawers, where it went on ticking away all by itself.

A mouse found it and laughed.

"What a fool! Nobody's here to look at you—so why do you keep on running?"

"Because I keep running even when no one is looking," the pocket watch replied, "I am useful whenever someone does look."

"Anyone who works only when no one is watching, or only when someone is watching—either way, that's a thief."

The mouse flushed with shame and scurried off without another word.

reasons.interest: The brief fable-like dialogue is witty and intellectually satisfying, ending in a neat moral reversal.
reasons.surprise: The mouse mocks the watch, but the watch’s sharp reply unexpectedly turns the accusation back on the mouse.
reasons.sadness: There is little tragedy; the watch’s isolation behind the drawers adds only a faintly forlorn note.
reasons.anger: The mouse’s sneering judgment may cause slight displeasure, but the tone is mostly light and corrective.

message.content: {"story_id":"T1","persona_id":"p0","language":"en","scores":{"interest":71,"surprise":58,"sadness":10,"anger":6},"reasons":{"interest":"The brief fable-like dialogue is witty and intellectually satisfying, ending in a neat moral reversal.","surprise":"The mouse mocks the watch, but the watch’s sharp reply unexpectedly turns the accusation back on the mouse.","sadness":"There is little tragedy; the watch’s isolation behind the drawers adds only a faintly forlorn note.","anger":"The mouse’s sneering judgment may cause slight displeasure, but the tone is mostly light and corrective."}}

Text identifier: T1
Title: The Pocket Watch
Author: Kyusaku Yumeno
Language of this prompt: en

Text:
A pocket watch had fallen behind a chest of drawers, where it went on ticking away all by itself.

A mouse found it and laughed.

"What a fool! Nobody's here to look at you—so why do you keep on running?"

"Because I keep running even when no one is looking," the pocket watch replied, "I am useful whenever someone does look."

"Anyone who works only when no one is watching, or only when someone is watching—either way, that's a thief."

The mouse flushed with shame and scurried off without another word.

reasons.interest: The brief fable is witty and intellectually pleasing, ending in a compact moral reversal.
reasons.surprise: The mouse’s mockery is neatly overturned by the watch’s reply, giving a modest but clear twist.
reasons.sadness: There is little real pathos, though the lonely image of the watch ticking unseen adds a faint melancholy.
reasons.anger: The text is mildly admonishing toward hypocrisy or laziness, but it provokes little actual indignation.

Text identifier: T1
Title: The Pocket Watch
Author: Kyusaku Yumeno
Language of this prompt: en

Text:
A pocket watch had fallen behind a chest of drawers, where it went on ticking away all by itself.

A mouse found it and laughed.

"What a fool! Nobody's here to look at you—so why do you keep on running?"

"Because I keep running even when no one is looking," the pocket watch replied, "I am useful whenever someone does look."

"Anyone who works only when no one is watching, or only when someone is watching—either way, that's a thief."

The mouse flushed with shame and scurried off without another word.

reasons.interest: The speaker’s implied stance centers on a witty moral exchange, making the moment mildly engaging and satisfying.
reasons.surprise: The central character likely experiences some novelty in being mocked by a mouse, then calmly overturning the insult with a sharp reply.
reasons.sadness: The watch seems isolated behind the drawers, but it shows steadiness rather than grief, so sadness is low.
reasons.anger: The watch answers composedly instead of resentfully, suggesting only slight displeasure at being called a fool.

Text identifier: T1
Title: The Pocket Watch
Author: Kyusaku Yumeno
Language of this prompt: en

Text:
A pocket watch had fallen behind a chest of drawers, where it went on ticking away all by itself.

A mouse found it and laughed.

"What a fool! Nobody's here to look at you—so why do you keep on running?"

"Because I keep running even when no one is looking," the pocket watch replied, "I am useful whenever someone does look."

"Anyone who works only when no one is watching, or only when someone is watching—either way, that's a thief."

The mouse flushed with shame and scurried off without another word.

reasons.interest: The watch’s witty moral reply suggests mild intellectual satisfaction and quiet amusement for the central speaking figure.
reasons.surprise: The talking watch and its sharp reversal of the mouse’s mockery create a modest sense of unexpectedness.
reasons.sadness: There is little real melancholy; only a faintly forlorn note in the watch being lost behind the drawers.
reasons.anger: The watch shows slight reproach in calling out the mouse’s logic, but no strong hostility or outrage.
